era_context: Late Bronze Age II. The major powers (Egypt, Hittites, Mycenae, Shang) are stable and prosperous. International trade networks are active. This is approximately 200 years before the Late Bronze Age collapse.

events.title: Ramesses II — reign continues
events.description: Ramesses II's reign continues. Egyptian power remains dominant in the eastern Mediterranean and Near East. Administrative, military, and building activities continue. The pharaoh is one of Egypt's longest-reigning and most prolific builders.

sources.name: Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Ramesses II's reign is well-attested; this year falls within it with high confidence.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for this year is minimal. The historical record is almost entirely dependent on Egyptian inscriptions. The vast majority of human activity and regions left no written record. The apparent 'stability' of this period is a function of the survival of elite, literate records.
